title: Generators
intro: Generation keeps Angular and backend in sync from the same contract source.

artifacts.name: contract-model.ts
artifacts.purpose: Typed endpoint model used by all generated outputs.
artifacts.whenToUse: Use this as the canonical type map when building custom integrations.
outputs: Shared typing source for client and backend generators.

artifacts.name: angular-client.ts
artifacts.purpose: Typed Angular client with method signatures per endpoint.
artifacts.whenToUse: Use in Angular apps to call APIs with contract-driven types.
outputs: Request/response inference without manual DTO duplication.

artifacts.name: zod-schemas.ts
artifacts.purpose: Runtime validation schemas mapped from contract shapes.
artifacts.whenToUse: Use in backend or gateway validation middleware.
outputs: Runtime safety aligned with contract definitions.

artifacts.name: nest-controller.ts
artifacts.purpose: NestJS controller skeleton and route mapping.
artifacts.whenToUse: Use to scaffold backend routes with minimal manual boilerplate.
outputs: Consistent route declarations from contract paths.

artifacts.name: nest-handlers.stub.ts
artifacts.purpose: Typed handlers scaffold for backend implementation.
artifacts.whenToUse: Use as implementation entrypoint for service business logic.
outputs: Clear compile-time contract boundaries for backend teams.

flowItems: Run generation and commit generated artifacts.
Wire generated outputs in Angular and backend.
readinessItems: Generated files are updated in the same commit as contract changes.
No manual edits are made inside generated output files.
Compatibility checks run in CI before merge.
Frontend/backend integration tests consume generated artifacts.
